slides.title: The Set 🧲
slides.content:
## Only Unique Items

A **Set** is an unordered collection that automatically destroys duplicates. It guarantees that every single item inside it is 100% unique.

Use `setOf()` or `mutableSetOf()` to create one.

quizData.question: If you try to `.add("Apple")` to a `MutableSet` that already contains "Apple", what happens?

options.text: You get a Set with two "Apple"s.
The command is silently ignored. Sets only keep one copy of each item.

quizData.explanation: Sets simply ignore duplicates. If the item exists, the `add()` command silently aborts.

slides.title: The Map 🗺️
slides.content:
## Dictionaries (Key-Value)

A **Map** (Dictionary in other languages) stores pairs of data: A `Key` and a `Value`.

You use the Key to quickly look up the Value. (Like entering a word in a dictionary to find the definition). Use `to` to link them together.

quizData.question: In the Map `mapOf("Lives" to 3, "Score" to 500)`, what is the Key necessary to retrieve the value 500?
quizData.explanation: The Key (`"Score"`) is the label/name you use functionally to retrieve the underlying Value (`500`).
